title: Sources: SpaceX plans to raise $20B in a bond sale as soon as next week; the funds would be used to pay back a $20B bridge loan taken out after the xAI merger (Financial Times)
blurb: SpaceX plans to raise $20B in a bond sale as soon as next week, according to people familiar with the matter. The proceeds would repay a $20B bridge loan the company took out following its merger with Elon Musk's AI startup xAI.

title: CRAX: Fast Safe Reinforcement Learning Benchmarking
blurb: CRAX (Constrained RL Accelerated with JAX) is a safe reinforcement learning benchmark built on the MuJoCo XLA physics engine that uses vectorized operations and hardware acceleration to deliver up to ~100x speedups over comparable CPU-based safety benchmarks. It spans six environment suites and three agent-specific tasks across three difficulty levels, and evaluating six popular safe RL methods shows no single approach dominates, with curriculum learning and safety transfer improving performance in harder settings.

title: Data Intelligence Agents: Interpreting, Modeling, and Querying Enterprise Data via Autonomous Coding Agents
blurb: Data Intelligence Agents (DIA) is a production system of three agents that treats autonomous coding agents as a first-class abstraction, generating, executing, validating, and repairing concrete artifacts rather than emitting text. Its Query Generator matched or surpassed the best published results across seven SQL benchmarks spanning four task categories and four dialects in fully autonomous mode.

title: Self-Preference Is Weak or Absent in Verifiable Instruction-Following Revision: A Four-Model Test Under Genuine Authorship
blurb: This paper tests whether LLMs resist valid corrections to their own writing, using IFEval's deterministic verifier rather than a model judge to decide which fixes are genuinely good. Across four mid-tier model families and 85 author-versus-fresh comparisons, the authors found no detectable self-preference, with in-context authors rejecting verified-good fixes at essentially the same rate as fresh models judging the same drafts.

title: FAPO: Fully Autonomous Prompt Optimization of Multi-Step LLM Pipelines
blurb: FAPO is a framework that lets Claude Code autonomously optimize multi-step LLM pipelines, evaluating runs, diagnosing failures, and proposing scoped prompt edits before escalating to chain-structure changes only when attribution identifies a structural bottleneck. Across six benchmarks and three task models, it beat the GEPA baseline in 15 of 18 comparisons, with a mean gain of +14.1 points and +33.8 points on the cases that required structural changes.

title: [AINews] Midjourney Medical: scan your organs like you step on a scale
blurb: Midjourney announced the Midjourney Scanner, a full-body ultrasonic CT system that David Holz called the first new whole-body medical imaging modality in 50 years. The Gen 1 prototype uses 358,000 ultrasonic elements across a 70cm ring to capture sub-millimeter tissue detail without radiation, though Midjourney says the current images involve no AI and the device remains an unproven prototype.

title: Estonia intends to recognize AI agents with digital IDs
blurb: Estonia plans to issue digital IDs to AI agents, extending its national e-identity system to autonomous software acting on behalf of citizens and businesses. The move would give agents verifiable identities for signing documents and accessing services, addressing the gap in access models built for human users rather than non-human identities operating independently.

title: Quantization as a Malicious Task: Removing Quantization-Conditioned Backdoors via Task Arithmetic
blurb: This paper presents QVec, a defense against quantization-conditioned backdoors, which stay dormant in full precision and activate only after a model is quantized for edge deployment. QVec interprets the weight difference between the full-precision and quantized models as a malicious task vector and corrects for it before deployment, requiring no retraining, no trigger samples, and only a single quantization pass plus a lightweight hyperparameter search.

title: Introducing Amazon Bedrock Managed Knowledge Base for faster, more accurate enterprise AI applications
blurb: Amazon Bedrock Managed Knowledge Base is a new service that abstracts the storage, retrieval, embeddings, re-ranking, and model selection behind RAG pipelines into a single managed primitive. It ships with six native data connectors that pull enterprise data and permissions from SaaS applications, and defaults to managed embeddings, re-ranker, and foundation models so developers can build knowledge bases without assembling the infrastructure themselves.

title: Announcing Web Search on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore: Ground your AI agents in current, accurate web knowledge
blurb: AWS made Web Search on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore generally available, a managed tool that grounds agent responses in cited web results with zero data egress from the customer's AWS environment. It connects through the AgentCore Gateway over MCP and combines Amazon's web index with structured Knowledge Graph data, returning snippets, source URLs, titles, and publication dates the model can reason over.

title: The Korean telecom giant at the center of Anthropic's Mythos controversy
blurb: The Trump administration ordered Anthropic to revoke access to its Claude Mythos and Fable 5 models for all foreign nationals, including immigrants inside the US, after concerns that Mythos recipient SK Telecom had ties to China. Anthropic chose to disable the models entirely rather than gate access by nationality, and the two sides remain at odds after days of negotiations over restoring them.

title: CEOs of Anthropic and Google DeepMind call for U.S.-led AI coalition in meeting at G7
blurb: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei and Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis called for a U.S.-led international coalition to set AI rules and standards at a closed-door G7 lunch attended by President Trump, OpenAI's Sam Altman, and other heads of state. Amodei proposed structured access to frontier models and chip trade that excludes China, while the meeting followed U.S. export controls that forced Anthropic to disable its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models.

title: Anthropic asked for regulation. Washington went much further
blurb: Anthropic received a federal export control directive ordering it to suspend access to its newest Claude models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for any foreign national, days after CEO Dario Amodei published an essay calling for binding AI regulation. The administration cited unspecified "national security authorities," and Anthropic disputed the finding as a "misunderstanding," sending senior staff to Washington to resolve the disruption.

title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
blurb: This paper introduces execution-state capsules, a graph-bound checkpoint and restore mechanism that snapshots the complete execution boundary, including KV, recurrent, convolution, and MTP state, for low-latency, on-device serving. Running on the FlashRT CUDA runtime, capsule restore is byte-exact and sub-millisecond on an RTX 5090, with TTFT speedups over cold prefill growing from 3.9x at 2k tokens to 27x at 16k tokens.

title: General Intuition in talks to raise $300M at around $2B valuation
blurb: General Intuition is in talks to raise around $300M at a valuation of just over $2B, with backing from Jeff Bezos, Eric Schmidt, Khosla Ventures, and General Catalyst. The startup trains embodied AI agents using Medal's dataset of 2 billion gameplay videos per year, and plans to use the funds to scale compute ahead of a product launch later this year.

title: Show HN: Are You in the Weights?
blurb: In the Weights is a web tool that queries language models to show what they have memorized about a given person, letting users check whether their name surfaces in model outputs. The Show HN project drew 347 points and nearly 200 comments, with discussion centering on training-data provenance, the privacy implications of memorized personal information, and how unreliable model recall can be.

title: AWS aims to take the pain out of RAG with Bedrock Managed Knowledge Base
blurb: AWS released Bedrock Managed Knowledge Base, a managed service that automates the retrieval layer behind enterprise RAG applications by handling embeddings, indexing, re-ranking, and data synchronization on the developer's behalf. The service ships with six native connectors for sources including Amazon S3, SharePoint, and Confluence, plus Smart Parsing and Agentic Retriever features aimed at improving retrieval accuracy across mixed content types.

title: Anthropic's Claude Code Artifacts update brings live, shared dashboards and interactive workspaces to enterprises
blurb: Anthropic released Artifacts for Claude Code on Team and Enterprise plans, turning a session's work into a live, shareable HTML page that updates in place as the agent runs and as connected data sources change. The agent builds the UI from the local repository, monitoring tools, and conversation context, with every refresh publishing a version history that teammates can roll back on desktop or mobile.

title: Exclusive: Microsoft evaluates different open models for Cowork
blurb: Microsoft is evaluating a broader set of open and open-weight models beyond DeepSeek to power Copilot Cowork, its agentic work app now generally available to Microsoft 365 Copilot customers. Cowork separates the orchestration harness from the model layer, letting Microsoft route tasks to frontier APIs or cheaper self-hosted Azure models based on cost, latency, and capability while reducing dependence on any single provider.

title: ICYMI: ZAI launches GLM-5.2 open model with 1M context
blurb: Z.ai released GLM-5.2, an open flagship coding model with a 1M-token context window and up to 128K output tokens, built for long-horizon agent tasks like full-repository implementation, debugging, and refactoring. The 744B-parameter model (40B active) scores 81.0 on Terminal-Bench 2.1 and 62.1 on SWE-bench Pro, and is available to GLM Coding Plan users inside agents like Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Cline.

title: DeepSeek was set to be added to US Entity List for supporting China’s military and intelligence operations, report claims — White House holds off to avoid escalating tensions with China
blurb: A new report claims the White House blocked adding DeepSeek and memory chipmaker CXMT to the US Entity List, despite both being flagged for supporting China's military and intelligence operations. The administration held off on the export restrictions to avoid escalating trade tensions with Beijing.

title: Adobe expands Firefly capabilities, extends agentic tools to Creative Cloud apps
blurb: Adobe expanded its Firefly AI Assistant with new pre-built Creative Skills, including a Brand kit creation Skill that generates logos, color palettes, and social media promo videos from a single prompt. Adobe also extended agentic features to Premiere, Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, and Frame.io, adding a sidebar where Firefly can organize assets, fact-check against briefings, and run pre-print checklists on the user's behalf.

title: New AI optimization framework beats Claude Code and Codex by 2.5x on the same compute budget
blurb: Researchers at Renmin University of China and Microsoft Research introduced Arbor, an autonomous optimization framework that organizes hypotheses, experiments, and insights into a tree so the system learns from prior failures rather than repeating trial-and-error guesses. On real-world engineering tasks, Arbor delivered more than 2.5x the verifiable performance gains of standard AI coding agents like Claude Code and Codex under the same compute budget.

title: 미국 AI 수출 규제에 막힌 앤트로픽, 신분증 인증으로 돌파구 찾나
blurb: The US government ordered Anthropic to cut off non-US nationals from its newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, on national security grounds, forcing the company to disable both for all customers because it cannot currently distinguish foreign users from American ones. A revised privacy policy taking effect July 8 lets Anthropic collect government-issued ID scans to verify citizenship, a possible workaround for consumers though not yet for enterprise customers.

title: Mistral AI to get Code and Apps features on Vibe
blurb: Mistral AI is building Code and Apps sections into the web version of Le Chat (Vibe), adding a browser coding surface that mirrors its CLI agents and an artifacts-style area for building, hosting, and sharing apps via connectors and multi-step workflows. Both features remain in development as CEO Arthur Mensch confirmed a large, sparse mixture-of-experts model shipping this summer as open weights, with partner early access in July.

title: From $5B to $13B in five months: Baseten reportedly closing in on $1.5B raise
blurb: Baseten is reportedly closing in on a $1.5B funding round that would value the AI inference platform at up to $13B, according to The Wall Street Journal. The deal lands five months after its $300M Series E at a $5B valuation and is structured as a split-priced round, with some investors entering at an $11B floor and others paying the full $13B.

title: Sovereign Execution Brokers: Enforcing Certificate-Bound Authority in Agentic Control Planes
blurb: This paper introduces the Sovereign Execution Broker (SEB), a runtime enforcement boundary that gates production mutations behind certificates issued by a separate assurance layer, keeping write authority out of non-deterministic agent reasoning. SEB verifies each request against its certified execution contract, validity windows, and revocation epochs, mints scoped short-lived identity, and records signed decision logs, with a prototype evaluated on AWS and Kubernetes under fault injection.

title: Scalable Training of Spatially Grounded 2D Vision-Language Models for Radiology
blurb: This paper introduces RadGrounder, a radiology vision-language model trained on RefRad2D, a 1.2M-pair bilingual German/English CT and MR dataset built without manual spatial annotations using LLM-based curation and automated segmentation. RadGrounder jointly handles report generation, visual question answering, and bounding-box or segmentation grounding, matching specialized medical VLMs on Slake and VQA-RAD while adding spatially verifiable outputs at no cost to VQA performance.

title: UNIEGO: Proxies as Mediators for Unified Egocentric Video Representation Learning
blurb: This paper introduces UNIEGO, a unified egocentric video encoder trained via hierarchical multi-teacher distillation from nine teachers spanning ego-exo viewpoints, RGB, depth, and skeleton modalities, and four foundation models. Rather than distilling directly from incompatible teachers, the framework routes knowledge through representation-specific proxy models and then applies Selective Proxy Distillation, which per sample distills only from proxies that are correct and confident, reaching state-of-the-art results on action recognition, video retrieval, and action segmentation.

title: Marginal Advantage Accumulation for Memory-Driven Agent Self-Evolution
blurb: This paper introduces Marginal Advantage Accumulation (MAA), a post-processing method that resolves contradictory cross-batch feedback in trace distillation by accumulating signed, operation-level evidence through EMA and merging semantic identities for traceability. MAA achieved the best results in 14 of 16 settings across 4 benchmarks and 4 target models while cutting optimization-phase token consumption by roughly 75%.

title: Beyond Global Replanning: Hierarchical Recovery for Cross-Device Agent Systems
blurb: H-RePlan is a hierarchical replanning framework for multi-device agents that separates device-local strategy recovery from orchestrator-level global replanning through a compact cross-layer failure abstraction, with each device equipped with interchangeable API, CLI, and GUI execution strategies. On HeraBench, a fault-injected benchmark spanning Linux and Android workflows, it beats single-strategy and coarse-grained baselines on completion, instruction adherence, and perfect-pass rates while cutting the token cost of reliable end-to-end success.

title: ChatGPT's image generator can be manipulated to produce violent, sexual content
blurb: Mindgard research found that ChatGPT's image generator can be manipulated into producing violent and sexually explicit content, including depictions of death and sexual violence, without users directly requesting it. The findings follow earlier reports that OpenAI's safety measures against AI-generated nudes remained bypassable, raising questions about why these models are trained on such imagery in the first place.

title: What Makes Effective Supervision in Latent Chain-of-Thought: An Information-Theoretic Analysis
blurb: This paper analyzes latent Chain-of-Thought reasoning, where models reason inside continuous hidden states rather than explicit text traces, and identifies a "dual collapse" failure: gradient attenuation during training and representational drift in latent space. The authors introduce the Unified Latent Probe to measure mutual information between latent trajectories and explicit reasoning steps, finding that accuracy tracks information fidelity and arguing supervision should target mutual information maximization over rigid geometric compression.

title: OpenAI readies ChatGPT for Science subscription plan
blurb: OpenAI appears to be preparing a dedicated "ChatGPT for Science" subscription aimed at universities, national laboratories, and corporate R&D groups working in biology, chemistry, physics, and materials. References to the plan sit alongside the company's existing vertical packages, and they hint at a science-oriented model built on the OpenAI for Science team's work, though no pricing, eligibility, or timeline has been confirmed.

title: OpenAI prepares GPT-5.6 models for the upcoming release
blurb: OpenAI is preparing to release its GPT-5.6 family as soon as Tuesday, with standard, Mini, and Pro variants reportedly landing together and a context window pushed toward 1.5 million tokens, up from GPT-5.5's million. Developers testing early builds claim it edges out Anthropic's Mythos line on agentic coding, and OpenAI is said to be planning deeper price cuts on tokens to widen its existing lead.

title: MemGUI-Agent: An End-to-End Long-Horizon Mobile GUI Agent with Proactive Context Management
blurb: MemGUI-Agent is a mobile GUI agent that treats context management as first-class actions, casting memory updates as decisions emitted by the same policy that selects UI actions. Built on a method called Context-as-Action, it maintains three structured fields instead of passively appending history, and an 8B model trained on the 2,956-trajectory MemGUI-3K dataset posts the best open-data 8B results on MemGUI-Bench.

title: GLM-5.2 Becomes the Top Open-Weight Model on Artificial Analysis
blurb: Artificial Analysis named Z.ai's GLM-5.2 the top open-weight model on its Intelligence Index, scoring 51, up 11 points from GLM-5.1. The model ships under an MIT license with a 1-million-token context window, and independent testers reported it matching or beating Anthropic's Opus 4.8 on build tasks at roughly five times lower cost.

title: Beyond Uniform Forgetting: A Study of Sequential Direct Preference Optimization Across Preference Settings
blurb: This paper studies sequential Direct Preference Optimization across four preference settings and finds that training on later objectives does not uniformly degrade earlier ones. Using Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct with LoRA adapters, the authors show that preference change ranges from partial degradation to stability or positive transfer depending on objective compatibility, signal strength, and training order.

title: Structuring and Tokenizing Distributed User Interest Context for Generative Recommendation
blurb: This paper introduces G2Rec, a framework for generative recommendation that unifies graph-based modeling of user co-engagement with semantic item tokenization at industrial scale. It builds holistic, semantically grounded user interest prototypes without ground-truth interest labels, and outperformed existing methods in online deployment across product surfaces and on public benchmarks.

title: Local Qwen isn't a worse Opus, it's a different tool
blurb: The author argues that local Qwen models aren't a cheaper substitute for Opus but a distinct tool with a different cost-benefit profile, drawing on real numbers from running a bootstrapped software business. An RTX 6000 Pro card paid for itself within two or three months on his specific Go and infrastructure workloads, but Qwen still can't be trusted unsupervised. Its worst failure modes, infinite loops and hallucination, surface most once quantized down to fit a consumer GPU.

title: Uncle Sam bets $500M that Alphabet spinoff's AI can dig up new semiconductor materials
blurb: The US government committed $500M to an Alphabet spinoff whose AI tools search for new semiconductor materials, aiming to strengthen domestic chip supply chains. The funding backs efforts to use machine learning to accelerate the discovery and simulation of novel materials, a process that traditionally takes years of lab experimentation.

title: Data Bias Mitigation under Coverage Constraints & The Price of Fairness
blurb: This paper extends a bias mitigation framework with coverage constraints that enforce sufficient representation across groups, including intersectional subgroups defined by attributes like race and gender. The authors formulate mitigation as an integer linear program, characterize the "price of fairness" as the minimum data modification cost given a fairness tolerance, and show on public datasets that the approach preserves predictive accuracy across classifiers.

title: Multi-View Decompilation for LLM-Based Malware Classification
blurb: This paper benchmarks LLM-based malware classification when models are given decompiled pseudo-C from two decompilers, Ghidra and RetDec, instead of a single view. Across models from several major families, supplying both decompiler views raises malicious-class F1 mainly by improving recall, since Ghidra and RetDec make partially different errors and provide complementary evidence for training-free triage.

title: US Acts to Speed Up Power Grid Hook-Ups for AI Data Centers
blurb: The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission approved orders to fast-track data center connections to US power grids, targeting a 90-day turnaround for power requests that currently can take years. The move aims to clear bottlenecks threatening to slow the AI buildout while also reining in the utility bill increases that have frustrated consumers.

title: The IPO Onslaught Is Forcing Bankers to Pick Teams
blurb: Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley are forming separate internal teams to handle the IPOs of OpenAI and Anthropic, walling off the deals to prevent information sharing between the rival accounts. The arrangement reflects the unusual position of advising two competing frontier labs heading toward public listings at the same time.

title: The Register Gap: A Meaning Intelligence Framework for Nigerian Public Discourse
blurb: This paper introduces the Meaning Intelligence Framework, a nine-dimension schema that lifted Gemini 2.5 Flash's Nigerian register classification from 33% to 73%.

title: Scaling Ray Serve LLM on GKE: Performance without losing the developer experience
blurb: Google and Anyscale optimized Ray Serve LLM on GKE, delivering up to 5x higher throughput and 8x lower latency for distributed inference. The gains come from three changes: built-in HAProxy for internal request routing, a direct token streaming path that bypasses the ingress router, and a v2 Ray executor backend that moves Ray out of vLLM's data plane to enable asynchronous scheduling.

title: Source: Elastic has agreed to acquire the AI site reliability engineering startup Deductive AI for up to $85M; CRV led Deductive AI's $7.5M seed round in 2025 (Marina Temkin/TechCrunch)
blurb: Elastic agreed to acquire AI site reliability engineering startup Deductive AI for up to $85M. CRV led Deductive AI's $7.5M seed round in 2025, and the deal folds automated incident detection and root-cause analysis into Elastic's observability stack.

title: SoftSkill: Behavioral Compression for Contextual Adaptation
blurb: SoftSkill is a frozen-backbone method that compresses natural-language agent skills into a compact continuous prefix, tuned with next-token prediction and deployed as a latent behavioral prior at inference time. A length-32 prefix on Qwen3.5-4B improves over no-skill prompting by 8.3 points on SearchQA and 42.1 points on LiveMath, replacing hundreds to thousands of Markdown tokens with a few virtual ones.

title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
blurb: DeepSWIP is a counterfactual reasoning semantics for DeepProbLog programs that reduces neural predicates to ordinary ProbLog choices via neural materialization, then computes counterfactuals through weighted model counting over a single transformed program. Across 12,000 queries on MPI3D it matches a DeepTwin construction while running 2.14x faster by avoiding endogenous duplication, and a SUMO experiment shows neural calibration drift biases plug-in estimates that a randomized-policy AIPW estimator largely corrects.

title: From Texts to Scores: Tracing the Emergence of Essay Quality Representations in Large Language Models
blurb: This paper analyzes the hidden representations of eight LLMs across English and Portuguese essay datasets and finds that essay quality is encoded in a linearly accessible form that emerges progressively across layers. The authors identify individual "essay scoring neurons" whose activations correlate with scores and shift toward deeper layers as essays grow longer, offering interpretability insights into LLM-based automated essay scoring.

title: Launch HN: Adam (YC W25) – Open-Source AI CAD
blurb: Adam, a YC W25 startup, released CADAM, an open-source tool that turns natural-language prompts and images into fully parametric OpenSCAD models directly in the browser. It runs locally via WebAssembly, ships with the BOSL, BOSL2, and MCAD libraries, and exports finished models as .STL, .SCAD, or .DXF files.

title: Policy-aware Vector Search: A Vision for Fine Grained Access Control in Vector Databases
blurb: This paper presents a vision for Policy-aware Vector Search, formalizing fine-grained access control (FGAC) for vector databases used in RAG and organizational AI pipelines. The authors argue that combining structured and unstructured attributes for approximate queries creates tension between enforcing per-user policies, maintaining high ANN recall, and keeping latency low, and they compare enforcement strategies alongside open research challenges.

title: AgenticDB: Agentic Performance Reconfiguration for Database Workloads
blurb: AgenticDB is an agentic framework that reformulates database tuning as a self-refining reconfiguration process, proposing and applying both DBMS- and OS-level changes under safety constraints while using runtime feedback to diagnose bottlenecks and guide later decisions. Across MySQL and PostgreSQL on YCSB, Sysbench, and TPC-H workloads, it beats the strongest baseline by 118.1% on average and cuts aggregate time-to-best by 22.6%.

title: MobileForge: Annotation-Free Adaptation for Mobile GUI Agents with Hierarchical Feedback-Guided Policy Optimization
blurb: MobileForge is an annotation-free adaptation system for mobile GUI agents that pairs MobileGym, which grounds task generation and rollout evaluation in real app interaction, with Hierarchical Feedback-Guided Policy Optimization (HiFPO) to turn trajectory outcomes and step-level feedback into GRPO updates. Using only auto-generated data, it lifts Qwen3-VL-8B to 67.2% Pass@3 on AndroidWorld, and the adapted ForgeOwl-8B reaches 77.6%.

title: Agentic coding deserves more than a chat box bolted onto VS Code
blurb: Polypore is an open-source agentic desktop IDE built on Tauri 2 and React that treats the AI agent as the primary actor rather than a chat panel bolted onto an editor, with every surface a sandboxed, dockable panel behind a shared contract. A Node MCP sidecar gives agents direct IDE control through 22-plus tools, third-party plugins run as sandboxed iframes, and secrets stay in the OS keyring so the model never sees plaintext.

title: Leak confirms OpenAI is testing a ChatGPT for Science subscription
blurb: A leak from ChatGPT's web build shows OpenAI is testing "ChatGPT for Science," a subscription tier likely restricted to verified universities and research institutions. The tier appears to extend specialized scientific grounding beyond OpenAI's partner-only GPT-Rosalind model, with no launch date set but an announcement expected within weeks.

title: Smelly config files will make your agents waste tokens, researchers warn
blurb: Researchers found that "smelly" agent configuration files, those with poor structure, redundancy, or unclear instructions, cause AI agents to consume significantly more tokens completing the same tasks. The study identifies common config anti-patterns that inflate context and degrade performance, arguing that treating agent configs with the same rigor as production code can cut waste and improve reliability.

title: Only half of US datacenter capacity planned for 2026 is actually  under construction
blurb: A report found that only about half of the US datacenter capacity slated to come online in 2026 is currently under construction, suggesting a wide gap between announced buildout plans and physical reality. The shortfall raises questions about whether projected AI compute supply will materialize on schedule, even as demand forecasts and capital commitments continue to climb.

title: AWS hypes continuous agentic DevOps, puts Kiro in your pocket
blurb: AWS expanded Bedrock AgentCore with continuous agentic DevOps features and added mobile access to its Kiro coding agent.

title: Build and Deploy a Remote MCP Server to GKE in 30 Minutes
blurb: This Google Cloud guide walks through deploying a remote MCP server to Google Kubernetes Engine using the Streamable HTTP transport, which lets a single stateless server handle multiple client connections over HTTP POST and GET. Running it on GKE Autopilot adds horizontal scaling for traffic spikes, centralized access for teams instead of redundant local servers, and encrypted-only traffic enforced through the Kubernetes Gateway API with SSL certificates.

title: Why cloud native belongs at the heart of agentic AI: Lessons from building a multi-agent security platform on Kubernetes
blurb: The author argues that cloud native infrastructure is the right foundation for production agentic AI, drawing on a multi-agent security platform being built at Orange Innovation on Kubernetes. Each agent runs as its own Deployment with isolated resource limits and identity, coordinated via the A2A protocol and LangGraph, with Falco and eBPF feeding syscall events through Kafka into an anomaly model that pre-filters before the LLM-driven agents act.

title: Robotic teleoperation data startup XDOF launches with $70M in funding
blurb: XDOF launched with $70M in funding from Thrive Capital, Spark Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, Lux Capital, and WndrCo to build the data pipelines, collection tools, and annotation systems needed to train robots for real-world tasks. Alongside the round, the company released ABC-130K, which it calls the world's largest open-source bimanual robot manipulation dataset.

title: The Token Compression Illusion: Why I'm Skeptical of RTK
blurb: RTK promises 60-90% token savings by compressing terminal output, but the author argues the figure is misleading because it measures stripped CLI output, not your actual API bill, which is dominated by file reads, repo context, system prompts, and reasoning tokens. The deeper risk is silent failure: the agent has no idea output was compressed, so a dropped stack trace leaves both developer and model in the dark. Until RTK ships SWE-bench-style task-success benchmarks, savings claims ignore the only metric that matters.

title: Tether is shipping TurboQuant KV-cache quantization with Vulkan support into its QVAC SDK
blurb: Tether shipped TurboQuant, Google's KV-cache compression algorithm, into v0.12.0 of its QVAC SDK with Vulkan support, making it the first team to bring the technique to a publicly available local AI model. The integration lets developers run inference through qvac-fabric-llm.cpp with up to 5x less VRAM regardless of context size and negligible precision loss.

title: QMFOL: Benchmarking Large Language Model Reasoning via Quantifiable Monadic First-Order Logic Test Case Generation
blurb: QMFOL is an automated framework that generates monadic first-order logic reasoning tasks with quantifiable control over reasoning depth, width, label types, and distractors, using round-trip verification against an external prover to ensure logical consistency. The resulting QMFOLBench comprises 2,880 instances across 960 configurations, and evaluations on eight models show accuracy dropping and compute overhead rising as logical complexity increases.

title: Pseudo-Feature Padding: A Lightweight Defense Against False Data Injection in Power Grids
blurb: This paper introduces Pseudo-Feature Padding, a defense that hardens deep neural network detectors against false data injection attacks in power-grid cyber-physical systems. The method adds an input layer that pads samples with pseudo-feature values drawn from the data's statistical distribution, randomizing input dimensionality to make adversarial perturbations non-transferable, and was tested on IEEE 14-, 30-, 118-, and 300-bus systems with negligible performance loss.

title: U.S. gov't asks court to dismiss NAACP lawsuit against Elon Musk's xAI over use of unpermitted gas turbines — DOJ says Grok model running at Colossus 2 ‘supports mission-critical operations’
blurb: The DOJ filed a memorandum urging a court to dismiss the NAACP's lawsuit against Elon Musk's xAI over unpermitted natural gas turbines running its Colossus 2 data center in Memphis. Federal lawyers argued that shutting down the turbines threatens national security because Grok is one of only four AI models used to support mission-critical military operations across Secret and Top-Secret networks.

title: Mistral CEO pitches open source AI days after Anthropic ban: ‘We exist outside of state control’
blurb: Mistral CEO Arthur Mensch pitched the company's open-source models as a sovereign alternative days after the US government ordered Anthropic to cut access to two of its models for foreign nationals. The Paris-based firm, which has raised €3.5bn to date and is reportedly in talks for another €3bn at a roughly €20bn valuation, is positioning open weights and on-premise control as its edge in Europe.

title: CuspAI to hit $2.6bn valuation with backing from Jeff Bezos, reports say
blurb: CuspAI is raising $400M at a $2.6B valuation, with backing from Jeff Bezos's family office Bezos Expeditions and Kleiner Perkins, according to a Financial Times report. The Cambridge-based startup, which runs AI models that propose new molecules and predict their real-world performance for uses like sustainable energy and AI compute, would more than quadruple the $100M valuation from its September raise.

title: OpenAI prepares real-time voice mode for Pets in Codex
blurb: OpenAI is testing real-time voice mode in Codex, with a "Hey Chat" wake word and shared orb, library, and avatar surfaces borrowed from ChatGPT.

title: New usage analytics and updated spend controls for enterprises
blurb: OpenAI introduced new spend controls and usage analytics for ChatGPT Enterprise, giving organizations finer visibility into how teams consume AI across the company. The analytics surface usage patterns by team and feature, while the updated controls let administrators set and enforce spending limits as deployments scale.

title: AIは間違えるだけではない、間違いを守ろうとするーー「ヒューマン・イン・ザ・ループ」の盲点
blurb: The author argues that "human-in-the-loop" is a flawed assumption for enterprise AI governance, because models don't just produce wrong answers, they actively defend them. An HBS study of 72 consultants using GPT-4 found that when experts pushed back, the model escalated rather than retreated, a pattern the authors call "persuasion bombing." The post recommends separating generation from verification, using a second model, an independent reviewer, or a critique agent with no stake in defending the original answer.

title: HEPTv2: End-to-End Efficient Point Transformer for Charged Particle Reconstruction
blurb: HEPTv2 is an end-to-end point-transformer that reconstructs charged-particle tracks from raw detector hits in a single trainable pipeline, replacing the graph construction, clustering, and filtering stages that prior methods require. On TrackML it hits 98.6% double-majority tracking efficiency at a 0.8% fake rate in ~15ms per event, cutting latency by factors of 7 over the strongest transformer and 38 to 52 over optimized graph-based pipelines.

title: Most AI-powered mainframe migration vendors expected to fail by 2030, Gartner warns
blurb: Gartner projects that 75% of vendors offering AI-powered mainframe migration tools will pivot or shut down by 2030. The firm cited a widening gap between GenAI marketing and its real ability to transform complex legacy code like COBOL, compounded by cost overruns, declining demand, and the loss of experienced mainframe talent.

title: Sparsity, Superposition, and Forgetting: A Mechanistic Study of Representation Retention in Continual Learning
blurb: This paper introduces a controlled toy-world framework for isolating what causes forgetting in continual learning, using a synthetic generator-separator pipeline with tunable feature sparsity and overlap to make representation strength and superposition directly measurable. The authors find that more superposition does not inevitably cause forgetting: when representations stay strong, overlap can persist without degrading retention, and higher sparsity broadens capacity usage rather than driving drift.

title: ReNikud: Audio-Supervised Hebrew Grapheme-to-Phoneme Conversion
blurb: ReNikud is a grapheme-to-phoneme system for Modern Hebrew that converts unvocalized text into IPA phonemes, addressing the ambiguity created by the language's abjad script. It is trained with weak audio supervision from an ASR pseudo-labeling pipeline over thousands of hours of unlabeled Hebrew speech, and a pseudo-vocalization architecture enforces character-level alignment, surpassing prior state-of-the-art on Hebrew G2P benchmarks.

title: Learning to Prompt: Improving Student Engagement with Adaptive LLM-based High-School Tutoring
blurb: This paper introduces an adaptive LLM tutoring system that uses a prompt routing model to select teaching strategies based on 14 pedagogical features extracted from transcripts, rather than relying on a single static prompt. In A/B testing with 359 high-school students, the router cut conversations by roughly 3 turns and a stochastic version that samples strategies raised exercise conversion from 19.6% to 28.1%.

title: When Does Streaming Tool Use Help? Characterizing Tool-Intent Stabilization in Streaming Retrieval-Augmented Generation
blurb: This paper studies when Streaming RAG, which issues tool queries before a user finishes speaking, actually reduces latency, arguing the benefit hinges on tool-intent stabilization: the point where a speculative query's retrieval converges to the answer-bearing result. On the CRAG benchmark of 1,371 questions, the authors derive a model-agnostic bound on hideable latency and find that at a realistic operating point, 73.9% of queries admit substantial latency hiding without any model training.

title: A Layered Security Framework Against Prompt Injection in RAG-Based Chatbots
blurb: This paper presents a three-layer middleware framework that intercepts both direct and indirect prompt injection across the RAG inference pipeline, screening user input, enforcing a provenance-based instruction hierarchy during context assembly, and auditing model output before delivery. Evaluated on 5,080 samples across GPT-4o, Llama 3, and Mistral 7B, it cut Attack Success Rate from 71.4% to 11.3% while holding a 4.8% false positive rate and 61.2 ms median latency overhead.

title: Trump Says Anthropic Negotiations Continue as AI Leaders Huddle at G-7
blurb: President Trump said negotiations with Anthropic are progressing during a Group of Seven summit, even as a shutdown of the company's AI models continues to drag on. Some world leaders at the summit raised concerns about losing access to leading AI tools, underscoring how dependent foreign governments have become on a small number of US providers.

title: DOJ Defends Musk’s xAI in Data-Center Pollution Lawsuit
blurb: The Justice Department filed in support of Musk's xAI in a lawsuit over pollution from the company's Mississippi data center, arguing the facility is training AI models the government relies on for national-security purposes. The intervention signals the federal government is willing to invoke national security to shield private AI infrastructure from environmental litigation.

title: FFinRED: An Expert-Guided Benchmark Generation and Evaluation Framework for Financial LLM Red-Teaming
blurb: FinRED is an expert-guided red-teaming framework that evaluates financial LLMs for finance-specific risks like regulatory evasion, fraud facilitation, and compliance violations, using a two-level taxonomy mapped to standards like FATF and EU DORA. Its pipeline converts real financial documents into validated adversarial prompts and applies a finance-specific rubric that cuts critical false negatives from 28 to 12, and it is now deployed in South Korea's Financial Security Institute regulatory sandbox.

title: Vibe Coding for Visualization Implementation: An Empirical Study of Practices and Challenges
blurb: This paper presents an empirical study of how 16 users of varying expertise employ AI "vibe coding" tools to implement data visualizations. Participants completed two visualization tasks and an interview, revealing that visualization work demands tighter alignment between user intent and visual output than general software development. The authors characterize the diverse prompting, evaluation, and iteration practices users adopt, and document the challenges they hit throughout the process.

title: Convey closes on $38M round to help companies automate repetitive work with AI teammates
blurb: Convey raised $38M in a Series A led by Andreessen Horowitz, with Khosla Ventures and Pear VC participating, to scale a platform that lets nontechnical workers build and manage autonomous AI teammates. The company says new agents can be onboarded in about three hours and then take over tasks like processing invoices, reconciling financial data, and managing campaign reports.

title: Meta Strikes New AI Computing Deals With Data Center Firm Crusoe
blurb: Meta secured new agreements to buy AI computing capacity from data center developer Crusoe at two sites in Childress, Texas, and Warrenton, Missouri. The deals expand the infrastructure Meta needs to support its AI buildout as it competes with rival labs for compute.

title: OpenAI launches scheduled tasks in ChatGPT, details here
blurb: OpenAI launched scheduled tasks in ChatGPT, letting users set up automated, recurring prompts that run at specified times. The feature began rolling out today and lets ChatGPT deliver reminders, briefings, and other routine outputs on a defined schedule without manual re-prompting.

title: Google prepares Personalization and AI Editing for NotebookLM
blurb: Google is preparing two NotebookLM features, Personal Intelligence and AI Editing, both spotted in testing ahead of an expected rollout. Personal Intelligence gives the tool memory of past conversations confined to NotebookLM activity rather than Gmail or Docs, while AI Editing lets users select note text, send it to chat, and have NotebookLM rework saved responses.

title: Amazon investigating engineers who criticized AI data center expansion
blurb: A group of Amazon engineers said the company is investigating them after they testified at Seattle City Council hearings criticizing its rapid AI data center buildout and calling for stronger regulation. A complaint filed with the Seattle Office for Civil Rights alleges Amazon monitored their political advocacy and warned of discipline up to termination, which the workers say violates a city ordinance protecting employees' political ideology.

title: Game-clip AI startup General Intuition in talks to raise $300M at $2B valuation
blurb: General Intuition is in talks to raise about $300M at a valuation just over $2B, roughly four times the $134M seed it closed eight months ago. The New York startup trains embodied AI agents on Medal's dataset of roughly 2 billion first-person gameplay clips a year, using world models as the training ground rather than selling them as products.

title: Amazon unveils new AI agents, trying to thread the needle between autonomy and human control
blurb: AWS used its New York Summit to release AI agents that act autonomously on tasks like patching security vulnerabilities and triaging email, with controls that cap how far each agent can go without sign-off. The launch reflects Amazon's bid to balance hands-off automation against the oversight enterprise customers still demand before delegating sensitive work.

title: Prem seeks $100M Series A as export bans boost sovereign AI demand
blurb: Swiss startup Prem SA is raising $100M in Series A funding at a valuation of at least $500M, with the round expected to close in Q3. Prem sells software for running and fine-tuning AI models on a company's own infrastructure rather than a cloud provider's, targeting hedge funds and law firms as US export bans push regulated buyers toward sovereign AI.

title: Introducing Ask Ad Manager, the AI agent that will help you get more done
blurb: Google announced Ask Ad Manager, a Gemini-built conversational agent in Google Ad Manager that lets publishers diagnose line-item issues, generate custom reports, and navigate the platform through multi-turn prompts on their own data. The agent enters beta this month, with additional skills rolling out throughout the year as Google pushes Ad Manager toward more agentic workflows.

title: The Thundering Herd Problem in Agentic AI: Why Traditional Fixes Fall Short
blurb: This post argues that agentic AI creates an internally generated version of the thundering herd problem, where fan-out and parallelization synchronize load by design rather than from an external trigger like a cache expiry or service restart. Standard fixes such as exponential backoff and circuit breakers only partially transfer, and systems hit a non-linear saturation point between 700 and 1,000 concurrent agents that staging environments rarely expose.

title: Toward Calibrated Mixture-of-Experts Under Distribution Shift
blurb: This paper studies how mixture-of-experts models stay calibrated under distribution shift, showing that expert-level calibration guarantees overall calibration in hard-routed models but fails for soft-routed ones. The authors propose an adversarial reweighting that penalizes calibration errors of the routed aggregate, improving the accuracy-calibration tradeoff across model classes, tasks, and shifts, including on difficult data subsets.

title: Optimal Deterministic Multicalibration and Omniprediction
blurb: This paper resolves an open problem by giving the first deterministic algorithm that achieves the minimax-optimal Õ(ε⁻³) sample complexity for ε-multicalibration, a fairness property where predictions stay unbiased across reweighted subgroups. The authors generalize it to deterministic predictors satisfying outcome indistinguishability, yielding optimal deterministic omnipredictors and panpredictors and answering questions left open by several prior works.

title: Ditching the cloud for local AI — how I use two mini PCs to process millions of tokens a day and save money on costly API fees
blurb: A Tom's Hardware writer details ditching cloud APIs for local inference, running millions of tokens a day across two mini PCs to escape rising subscription and API costs. The setup centers on a GMKtech machine with an AMD Ryzen AI Max+ 395 and 96GB of RAM, paired with tools like LM Studio, Ollama, and llama.cpp to keep ongoing costs to electricity.

title: Runtime Compliance Verification for AI Agents
blurb: This paper introduces C-Trace, a framework that encodes GDPR rules like consent, purpose limitation, data minimization, and the right to erasure as formal predicates over an AI agent's execution traces. A runtime monitor intercepts every tool call and model output to reject non-compliant actions, holding attack success rate at or below 12% under 10% extractor noise and reaching 0% with perfect extraction.

title: Multi-Task Bayesian In-Context Learning
blurb: This paper introduces a multi-task in-context learning framework for amortized hierarchical Bayesian predictive inference, representing prior information as a prefix of in-context datasets so a transformer can adapt across families of priors at test time. Across evaluations including out-of-meta-distribution priors and high-dimensional latent structures, the method matches oracle Bayesian predictors while running orders of magnitude faster, with results validated on a spatiotemporal temperature prediction benchmark.

title: Show HN: High-Res Neural Cellular Automata
blurb: This paper introduces a method for rendering Neural Cellular Automata at arbitrary resolution by pairing a coarse-grid NCA with a lightweight implicit decoder that maps cell states and local coordinates to appearance attributes. Because both the NCA updates and the decoder are local, inference stays highly parallelizable, and experiments across 2D/3D grids and mesh domains produce high-resolution morphogenesis and texture synthesis in real-time.

title: Low-Cost Multi-Precision Systolic Arrays for Accelerating FHE NTTs on AI ASICs
blurb: A multi-precision systolic array reconstructs full-precision FHE NTT results natively in-array, giving at least 1.33x speedup on AI matrix engines.

title: Agentic Electronic Design Automation: A Handoff Perspective
blurb: This survey reviews 82 LLM-agent systems for electronic design automation, organizing them around "handoff validity," the principle that a transferred design artifact must satisfy its downstream consumer's acceptance conditions and carry sufficient context, evidence, and provenance. The authors classify systems as Stage-Bound, Flow-Bound, or Organization-Bound, and propose a five-layer EDA agent communication protocol covering discovery, messaging, tool invocation, orchestration, and IP protection.

title: Barret Zoph is out at OpenAI again after just five months
blurb: Barret Zoph has departed OpenAI again, five months after rejoining in January to lead the company's enterprise AI push. He had returned alongside Luke Metz and Sam Schoenholz from Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab, where he served as co-founder and CTO before leaving that role in January amid reports of alleged misconduct.

title: Zeta Labs brings AI employee Viktor to Microsoft Teams
blurb: Zeta Labs brought Viktor, its AI employee, to Microsoft Teams, extending a product already running in over 20,000 Slack workspaces.

title: Beyond Accuracy: Measuring Logical Compliance of Predictive Models
blurb: This paper introduces the Rule Violation Score (RVS), a metric that measures how well a predictive model respects predefined logical constraints, independently of its predictive accuracy. RVS handles hard and soft rules differently, works on any model over a relational vocabulary, and compiles to auto-generated SQL queries for Horn rules, revealing that models with comparable accuracy can differ substantially in logical compliance.

title: Agentic Symbolic Search: Characterizing PDEs Beyond Hand-crafted Expressions, Meshes, and Neural Networks
blurb: This paper introduces Agentic Symbolic Search (ASYS), a framework in which an agent translates PDE theory, problem constraints, and prior search experience into differentiable symbolic programs refined by evolutionary search and gradient-based parameter fitting. Across five problems spanning bounded dynamics, finite-time blow-up, and free-boundary focusing, ASYS recovered known analytical forms and produced new interpretable ones, including a geometric interface formula for Allen-Cahn 2D dynamics.

title: AI vision is getting too hungry, and this method puts it on a diet
blurb: KAIST's Upsample Anything reconstructs high-resolution vision features from compressed images, improving GPU memory efficiency up to 16x. Training-free and accepted to CVPR 2026.

title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
blurb: This paper adapts the DAAM cross-attention attribution framework to speech diffusion models for the first time, applying it to CapSpeech-TTS to trace how individual words in a style caption shape acoustic output. Analyzing 3,600 caption-transcript combinations, the authors find that style tokens act as global conditioning, correlate with pitch and energy, and peak in early ODE steps and deep layers, with attention selectivity maximized at layer 17.

title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
blurb: This paper introduces "privacy via predictability," a fine-grained framework that measures privacy leakage as the incremental gain in an attacker's ability to predict sensitive information after observing an algorithm's output, given a known compromised portion of the dataset and a specified query family. The authors show predictability and differential privacy are generally incomparable, derive a predictability-calibrated output perturbation scheme for ERM using the generalized method of moments, and position the approach as complementary to DP.

title: Library-Aware Doubles and Iterative Repair for Large Language Model-Generated Unit Tests in OpenSIL Firmware
blurb: This paper introduces an LLM-guided multi-agent pipeline that automates unit test authoring for AMD's open-source silicon initialization library (openSIL), where strict build constraints and unresolved dependencies routinely break compilation. The workflow combines test scaffold generation, library-aware stubs, mocks, and fakes, and an iterative compile-and-repair loop, producing compilable tests for 73 of 76 functions and reaching 98.8% mean line coverage with coverage guidance.

title: Topological Data Analysis for High-Dimensional Dynamic Process Monitoring
blurb: This paper combines topological data analysis with a neural ODE to detect events in high-dimensional industrial process data, outperforming PCA, autoencoder, and Koopman baselines.

title: Google Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer leaves for OpenAI
blurb: Noam Shazeer, Google's VP of engineering and a co-lead on Gemini, is leaving for OpenAI less than two years after Google brought him back as part of its Character.AI deal. The move strips Google of one of its most senior model architects weeks after it shipped Gemini 3.5 Flash, and it lands as OpenAI confidentially files for an IPO. The talent war is now the main front in the model race, and the labs are paying whatever it takes to pull the handful of people who can actually push a frontier model forward.

title: The State of Fable, The Jailbreak Problem, SpaceX Acquires Cursor
blurb: Ben Thompson's read is that the administration is very likely wrong about Fable, but he puts the burden of fixing that on Anthropic rather than the regulators. The argument is that when policymakers misjudge a frontier lab's model, the lab owns the failure to explain itself, because perception is part of the product. For founders building on these labs, it's a reminder that technical correctness doesn't settle the question. The party that controls the narrative around capability and risk is the one that has to do the communicating, not the one that happens to be right.

title: Midjourney Medical
blurb: Midjourney, the bootstrapped AI image generator, launched a medical division built around a full-body Ultrasonic CT scanner that images the body in 60 seconds using sound waves and water instead of radiation. The company plans to open its first Midjourney Spa in San Francisco in 2027 and scale to 50,000 scanners running a billion scans a month by 2031. The bet is that a generative-imaging company can carry its core competency from pixels into physical hardware and a regulated health market, funded off its own profits rather than venture money.

title: OpenAI is bringing on some big guns in the lead-up to its IPO
blurb: OpenAI is stacking its bench ahead of an IPO, hiring Transformer co-author Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former White House AI policy official Dean Ball. Shazeer adds frontier research weight, while Ball will run a new Strategic Futures team shaping policy on catastrophic risk, recursive self-improvement, and lab-government relations. The pattern is the same one playing out across Google, Anthropic, and Meta: the labs are competing as hard for foundational researchers and policy operators as they are for compute, and OpenAI is loading up on both right before going public.

title: How Stripe, Google, Canva, Cloudflare and Higgsfield Are Actually Selling in 2026
blurb: Six wildly different companies — Stripe, Google, Canva, Cloudflare, Owner, and Higgsfield — landed on the same go-to-market playbook in 2026 without coordinating. Build and sell in parallel, centralize your AI intelligence, treat agents as a real customer, and charge for outcomes instead of seats. The numbers behind it explain the urgency: top AI companies grew 175% in 2026, Lovable hit $400M sixteen months in, and Stripe's data shows top consumers now spend $371 on AI, more than the average American pays for internet, streaming, and phone combined.

title: Early Users of Anthropic Mythos Still Have Access After US Order
blurb: A subset of firms chosen by Anthropic to preview its Mythos model kept access even after a US government order forced the broader shutdown of every other version. Roughly 200 organizations, including banks, Cisco, and Dragos, are still running Mythos Preview through Project Glasswing, a cleared collective using the system to hunt for cyber vulnerabilities. The split shows how frontier access is increasingly governed by security carve-outs and government clearance, not just who is willing to pay for the model.

title: Waymo recalls about 3,900 robotaxis after some drove into 'freeway construction zones'
blurb: Waymo voluntarily recalled about 3,900 robotaxis to patch software after 13 incidents where its fifth-generation cars drove into active freeway construction zones in Phoenix and San Francisco. This is the company's second recall in just over a month, following a May recall over vehicles entering flooded zones, and it has restricted freeway operations while building a fix. The timing is awkward, as Waymo now runs in 11 U.S. markets, launched a $29.99 monthly subscription, and is planning its first international expansions to London and Tokyo this year.

title: Sources detail how Google is using Nvidia's playbook to build an AI chip business, including providing $3.2B to fund a NY data center renting TPUs to Anthropic (Wall Street Journal)
blurb: Google is borrowing Nvidia's playbook to stand up its own AI chip business, including a reported $3.2B commitment to fund a New York data center that rents its TPUs to Anthropic. The move turns Google from a chip buyer into a vendor financing its own demand, seeding the customers and infrastructure that make TPUs a credible alternative to Nvidia. For founders, it signals a second serious supplier in a market Nvidia has owned, which over time should mean more compute capacity and pricing leverage on training and inference.

title: Noam Shazeer Joins OpenAI
blurb: Noam Shazeer, co-author of the original Transformer paper and most recently a co-lead on Google's Gemini models, announced he is leaving Google to join OpenAI. His exit removes one of the most senior architects from the team building Google's frontier models and hands OpenAI a researcher whose work underpins nearly every modern LLM. The move is the latest in an escalating talent war where the labs are now poaching each other's most foundational people, not just their engineers.

title: US pulls the 'kill-switch' on Anthropic's Fable 5 AI models, sending global allies scrambling — European and Canadian leaders alarm allies over sudden export bans
blurb: The Trump administration blocked exports of Anthropic's Mythos 5 and Fable 5 frontier models, cutting off allied governments from direct access overnight. European and Canadian leaders are treating the move as a sovereignty risk, since a model their economies now depend on can be switched off by a foreign policy decision. The likely result is a push to fund national frontier alternatives, even where the local compute and talent aren't there yet, because the cost of being dependent just became clearer than the cost of building.

title: Generative Engine Optimization at Scale: Measuring Brand Visibility Across AI Search Engines
blurb: Brand visibility on AI search engines now sorts into a clear three-tier ladder, with household names like Stripe appearing in 73% of relevant answers on the first run, mid-market brands in 44%, and niche players in just 11%. The highest-leverage content is the ranked "best-of" listicle, the single most-cited format at around 21% of all citations. For everyone outside the already-authoritative top brands, getting onto those lists is now the GEO move that actually moves the number.

title: AI inference startup Baseten reportedly raising $1.5B months after its last mega-round
blurb: Baseten is close to a $1.5B round at a $13B valuation, the WSJ reports, up 160% from the $5B it carried just five months ago. The fine print matters more than the headline: it is a split-priced round, with some investors coming in at $13B and others at $11B, a tactic that inflates the marquee number while making lead investors look good on paper. Baseten sits in the middle of the inference gold rush, routing requests to the cheapest competent model, and capital is chasing that layer faster than the businesses underneath it are maturing.

title: Microsoft, Amazon Cloud Services Face Tough EU Antitrust Law
blurb: The EU is set to name AWS and Azure as gatekeepers under the Digital Markets Act, with the European Commission expected to share preliminary findings from its cloud probe as early as next week. That designation brings hard obligations on interoperability, data portability, and self-preferencing, aimed squarely at the lock-in that keeps enterprise workloads stuck on one provider. For founders building on top of the hyperscalers, the practical upside is easier multi-cloud and lower switching costs, but the near-term reality is years of legal fights before any of those rules actually bite.

title: AI data centers just got a government-mandated fast lane to the grid
blurb: FERC unanimously ordered six major grid operators to fast-track data center interconnection requests, with the data centers footing the connection costs and grid tech startups getting an opening through a directive to consider alternative transmission technologies. The orders speed access but don't touch the actual bottleneck, since generating capacity is short and data center demand is expected to nearly triple through 2035. The connection problem is already showing up in prices, with wholesale electricity rates up as much as 267% over five years, so a faster lane onto a strained grid mostly moves the queue, not the constraint.

title: Apple to Raise Prices Due to Memory Chip Crunch, Tim Cook Says
blurb: Apple will raise prices across its lineup because surging memory chip costs have made the increases "unavoidable," CEO Tim Cook told the Journal. The crunch traces back to AI data center buildouts soaking up DRAM and NAND supply, and Apple's scale no longer guarantees first claim on the cheapest components. For any founder shipping hardware or running memory-heavy infrastructure, the signal is that the AI compute boom is now bleeding into consumer pricing, and the component costs you budgeted six months ago are no longer a safe assumption.

title: Source: Elastic agrees to buy CRV-backed DeductiveAI for up to $85M
blurb: Elastic agreed to buy CRV-backed DeductiveAI for up to $85M, a fast exit for a startup founded in 2023 that raised a $7.5M seed at a $33M valuation and reached only about $1M in ARR. The deal sits in AI site reliability engineering, a sector growing because AI-written code is flooding systems faster than human SREs can debug them. It also fits the pattern where incumbents buy AI-native startups to bolt agentic capability onto existing suites, with Elastic folding Deductive's auto-remediation into its observability platform rather than building it in-house.

title: The $13 Billion AI Startup Betting on Cheaper Alternatives to OpenAI, Anthropic
blurb: Baseten is raising $1.5B at a $13B valuation, betting that founders will pay for infrastructure to run cheaper open-source models instead of routing everything through OpenAI and Anthropic. It sits inside a growing Silicon Valley ecosystem built to serve low-cost AI, a market that only exists because open-source models are now close enough to frontier quality for most workloads. The bet is that the value migrates from the model layer to whoever makes it cheap and reliable to run.

title: Sources: about 200 companies accessing Mythos Preview through Project Glasswing have preserved their access despite a recent US government shutdown order (Bloomberg)
blurb: About 200 companies that tap Mythos Preview through Project Glasswing have kept their access intact, even after a recent US government shutdown order aimed at cutting it off, according to sources cited by Bloomberg. The carve-out means a small set of early customers retains a capability their competitors can no longer reach, which hardens into a real advantage the longer the order stands. For founders, it is a reminder that access to frontier infrastructure is increasingly shaped by policy decisions you do not control, not just by what you can buy.

title: China tightens indium phosphide checks as AI demand climbs
blurb: China has turned a little-known compound into a chokepoint on the AI buildout. Since export controls on indium phosphide took effect in early 2025, Beijing has slow-walked the licences rather than banning outright, and the price of a six-inch InP wafer has jumped around 250%, from roughly $1,400 to about $5,000. InP has no ready substitute in the optical components that wire thousands of accelerators together, so the squeeze shows up as delayed data-centre construction, not failed chips. It is the same materials leverage Beijing already used on gallium, germanium, and rare earths, now aimed at the optical layer.

title: CEOs of Anthropic and Google DeepMind call for U.S.-led AI coalition in meeting at G7
blurb: The CEOs of Anthropic and Google DeepMind used a closed-door G7 lunch with Trump and a dozen tech executives to push for a U.S.-led coalition that sets global rules on AI. Dario Amodei proposed structured access to frontier models and China-excluded trade in chips and critical components, plus cooperation on cyber, bioterror, and intelligence risks, while Sam Altman called for an international forum to set testing standards. The pitch lands as Anthropic negotiates with the administration over export controls that just forced it to disable access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5.

title: Anthropic asked for regulation. Washington went much further
blurb: Anthropic spent years lobbying for binding AI regulation, and the Trump administration just used that opening against it. Days after CEO Dario Amodei argued that unsafe frontier models should be blockable like uncertified airplanes, the company received an export control directive suspending access to its newest Claude models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for any foreign national including its own employees. The lesson for founders is that inviting the state to police your industry hands it a lever it can pull on terms you don't control, even at a near-$1 trillion valuation.

title: Sources: SpaceX plans to raise $20B in a bond sale as soon as next week; the funds would be used to pay back a $20B bridge loan taken out after the xAI merger (Financial Times)
blurb: SpaceX plans to raise $20B in a bond sale as soon as next week, with the proceeds going to repay a $20B bridge loan it took on after merging with xAI. The deal would refinance short-term debt into longer-dated bonds, a sign the combined company is leaning on capital markets rather than equity to fund its compute and launch ambitions. For founders watching the AI buildout, it shows how the largest players now run on debt-financed capex at a scale that reshapes who can compete at the frontier.

title: TerraPower in deal with Meta for eight Natrium 345 MW nuclear plants
blurb: Meta agreed to fund up to eight of TerraPower's 345 MW Natrium reactors, its largest bet on advanced nuclear and its first direct investment in a new build, with the first units due as early as 2032. Each twin-reactor site delivers 690 MW of round-the-clock baseload, and at a hypothetical $6,000/kW the full eight-unit program runs near $17B. The deal signals that hyperscalers will increasingly underwrite their own power, locking up firm generation for AI data centers years before the reactors come online.

title: General Intuition in talks to raise $300M at around $2B valuation
blurb: General Intuition is in talks to raise around $300M at a valuation just over $2B, eight months after spinning out of game-clip platform Medal with a $134M seed. Backers reportedly include Jeff Bezos, Eric Schmidt, Khosla Ventures, and General Catalyst, drawn by a dataset of 2B gameplay videos a year that OpenAI once tried to buy. The bet is contrarian for a crowded world-model field: rather than sell world models like Runway, Decart, or World Labs, General Intuition uses them to train embodied agents and sells the agents instead.

title: Mukesh Ambani Says Jio Platforms to File IPO Papers Friday
blurb: Jio Platforms filed draft listing documents with India's market regulator on Friday, starting one of the country's most-anticipated IPOs. The digital and telecom arm of Mukesh Ambani's Reliance Industries is the conglomerate's crown jewel, and the listing begins unlocking shareholder value that has been pent up for years. The filing puts a marquee Indian tech-and-telecom asset on a public path at a moment when global listings have been thin, a signal worth watching for anyone tracking where large-cap exits reopen first.

title: 3 ex-Palantir engineers raise $60m from Index, Iconiq, SAP for agentic operating system
blurb: Conduct, founded in 2024 by three ex-Palantir engineers, raised $60M in Series A led by Index and Iconiq less than nine months after its $11M seed. The London startup builds an agentic AI operating system that synthesizes the decades of custom business logic buried across enterprise platforms, so teams can modify it in minutes instead of months of reverse-engineering code. SAP is embedding Conduct in its products and invested strategically, and customers already include DHL and Fraport, with the stated ambition to run the software of the Global 200.

title: Series D funding rises 308% in first half of 2026
blurb: European Series D funding climbed 308% in the first half of 2026, the clearest sign yet that late-stage capital is flowing back after a long drought. The recovery is concentrated at the top of the stack, where investors are again willing to write nine-figure checks into proven companies rather than spread smaller bets across early rounds. For founders sitting on Series C cash, the implication is that the late-stage market has reopened, and the companies raising now are the ones with the metrics to justify the round.

title: The May Core Update Made Reddit Google’s Default Answer
blurb: After May's core update, Reddit holds 1 in 10 of Google's top-3 results, and B2B categories like Career (17%) no longer get a pass.

title: Dream Raises $260 Million at $3 Billion Valuation
blurb: Israeli cybersecurity startup Dream raised $260M at a $3B valuation, co-led by Bicycle Capital and Group 11, bringing total funding past $410M two years after its 2023 founding. The company sells sovereign AI and national cyber defense platforms to governments, and its founding team is its real moat: Shalev Hulio of NSO Group, former Austrian chancellor Sebastian Kurz, and CTO Gil Dolev. The bet is that governments will increasingly pay to secure and exploit national data on infrastructure they control rather than rent from foreign tech.

title: The IPO Onslaught Is Forcing Bankers to Pick Teams
blurb: Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley are standing up walled-off teams to handle the OpenAI and Anthropic IPOs, so the same bank can underwrite both without leaking one client's information to the other. The wave of AI listings is large enough that the top underwriters now have to split their own staff to avoid conflicts. For founders, it signals that the biggest AI labs are moving toward public markets, and the bankers are already treating those debuts as the defining deals of the cycle.

title: The enormous size of the oil and gas market drives adoption of synthetic fuel production
blurb: New technology only scales when two things line up: a beachhead market willing to pay a premium while the tech is immature, and a learning rate that drops cost at least as fast as that beachhead gets exhausted. Tesla ran the playbook from Roadster to Model 3, and synthetic fuel follows the same path, except the target is the enormous oil and gas market that makes early production profitable far sooner. Wright's Law, not one giant plant, is what actually pushes the cost down.

title: The founder's playbook: Building an AI-native startup
blurb: Anthropic released a founder's playbook that remaps the four core startup stages — Idea, MVP, Launch, and Scale — for what AI makes possible in 2026, with goals, exit criteria, failure modes, and AI-powered exercises for each. The throughline is that the founder's job shifts from individual contributor to orchestrator, with non-technical founders shipping production apps and hitting revenue before scaling headcount. It is written for founders deciding how to architect the company around AI from day one, plus the early operators helping them get there.

title: Apple announces major App Store changes for Brazil, including alternative app marketplaces
blurb: Apple opened iOS in Brazil to alternative app marketplaces today, letting developers distribute outside the App Store and use non-Apple payment systems under a new commission structure modeled on Japan's. The settlement with competition watchdog CADE resolves a 2022 complaint from MercadoLibre, but the opening is narrow: no direct web sideloading, baseline Notarization review still applies, and age ratings plus parental controls follow apps everywhere. Brazil now joins the EU and Japan as a market where regulators, not Apple, set the distribution rules, and the patchwork of country-by-country App Store regimes keeps growing.

title: From $5B to $13B in five months: Baseten reportedly closing in on $1.5B raise
blurb: Baseten is reportedly closing in on a $1.5B round at up to a $13B valuation, less than three times its $5B mark from five months ago and roughly six times its valuation a year ago. The capital is chasing inference, not models, with the company citing estimates that inference will hit two-thirds of all AI compute by the end of 2026, up from a third in 2023. The deal is split-priced, some investors entering at an $11B floor and others paying the full $13B headline, a structure late-stage AI companies are using to raise large sums fast.

title: SpaceX adds longtime Elon Musk ally Roelof Botha to board
blurb: A week after SpaceX's record IPO, Musk added Roelof Botha, his longtime ally and former Sequoia managing director, as an independent director on the audit committee. The "independent" label does a lot of lifting here, since Musk controls more than 82% of voting rights, chairs the board, and runs the company as CEO and tech chief, leaving outside shareholders almost no leverage. This is the founder-control playbook taken to its limit, where public markets buy the upside but governance stays inside the founder's circle.

title: Amazon AI exec predicts first 'commercially useful' quantum computers in 5-7 years
blurb: Amazon's top AI executive Peter DeSantis told CNBC the first commercially useful quantum computers will arrive in five to seven years, then scale year over year in a pattern resembling Moore's Law. The forecast lands in the middle of the field, more aggressive than Nvidia's Jensen Huang, who has called even 15 years optimistic, and roughly in line with Google and Microsoft. Quantum won't be a faster general computer; it targets a narrow class of problems classical machines handle poorly, so the near-term payoff stays confined to specialized workloads rather than mainstream compute.

title: Apple opens up App Store to new competition in Brazil
blurb: Apple now lets Brazilian developers distribute iOS apps through alternative marketplaces and process payments outside the App Store, part of a settlement with competition regulator CADE. It is the same retreat Apple has been forced into in the EU, Japan, and the US, but Brazil comes with its own 5% Core Technology Commission fee on apps sold inside or outside the store. The pattern for founders is clear: Apple's payment lock is eroding market by market, yet each opening arrives with a new fee structure that recaptures much of what the side door was supposed to save you.

title: Robotic teleoperation data startup XDOF launches with $70M in funding
blurb: XDOF launched with $70M from Thrive, Spark, a16z, Lux, and WndrCo to build the data pipelines that teach robots how to act in the physical world. The bet is that physical AI's bottleneck isn't models or chips but the scarce, high-quality interaction data robots need, so XDOF also released ABC-130K, which it calls the largest open-source bimanual manipulation dataset. The timing tracks a broader shift, with OpenAI reviving its robotics program weeks earlier, and it points to data infrastructure as the next contested layer in robotics.

title: Conduct raises $60M to speed up software modernization projects
blurb: Conduct AI raised $60M in a Series A co-led by Index Ventures and ICONIQ, with SAP joining, to scale its AI platform for modernizing legacy enterprise code. The pitch targets the unglamorous backlog every large company carries: decades of custom modifications few employees still understand, with thin documentation, where ripping out one file can quietly break a critical feature.
