blurb: Meta added expanded analytics, AI restyle options, sticker and text opacity controls, and new audience insights to Edits, its standalone video editing app. The comparative data and audience features give short-form creators and brand teams clearer signal on which edits drive watch-through, tightening the loop between production choices and Reels performance.

title: Generative Engine Optimization at Scale: Measuring Brand Visibility Across AI Search Engines
blurb: A new analysis of 100K+ AI-search responses across 100+ brands quantifies how visibility splits by brand maturity: global names like Stripe and Nike appear in 73% of relevant answers, mid-market brands in 44%, and niche or early-stage brands in just 11%. Citations concentrate where marketers can act, with 78% pointing to corporate sites and ranked "best-of" listicles the single most-cited format at 21%. Sentiment is the volatile signal, flipping 6.7x more often than whether a brand gets mentioned at all.

title: Instagram now lets you add a unique caption to each carousel slide
blurb: Instagram rolled out per-slide captions for carousels, letting users write a unique caption for each image instead of one shared caption across the whole post. Combined with the earlier bump to 20 photos per carousel, the change gives social teams more room to structure multi-image stories and sequence a narrative across slides rather than front-loading everything into a single caption.

title: How Attention.com Turns Sales Calls Into Pipeline: The Best GTM Data You Own, and Why Most B2B Teams Throw It Away
blurb: At SaaStr AI 2026, Attention.com CEO Anis Bennaceur argued that next quarter's pipeline already sits in the sales calls you recorded this quarter and never mined. His core play: rebuild your ICP monthly off live closed-won data instead of annually, using an agent that pulls recent wins, extracts buyer firmographics and tenure from call transcripts, and enriches them in a tool like Clay. The output is a dozen narrow buyer pockets you can write real emails to, like "the frustrated veteran stuck on an incumbent," rather than one stale broad ICP.

title: LinkedIn adds more digital skills visibility
blurb: LinkedIn now lets users surface verifiable skills pulled from more than a dozen platforms, including HubSpot, Fiverr, and Beehiiv, directly on their profiles. For marketers, this turns LinkedIn profiles into a richer credentialing signal, giving demand-gen and social teams sharper targeting data for skills-based audience segmentation and partner sourcing.

title: Your Instagram photo dumps just got a caption for every single slide
blurb: Instagram now lets creators add a unique caption to every slide in a carousel post, supporting up to 20 distinct captions per post starting today. The change turns a single carousel into a micro-blog, letting brands attach product details, tutorial steps, or context to each image rather than cramming one caption across up to 20 photos.

title: How Unilever Is Turning Its 2026 FIFA World Cup Sponsorship Into a 50,000-Creator Content Engine
blurb: Unilever is turning its FIFA World Cup 2026 sponsorship into one of its largest marketing programs, activating 35-plus brands across 120-plus markets with more than 50,000 creators producing cross-channel content over the 39-day event. The company is framing the deal as a commercial platform rather than brand visibility, tying creator content to retail distribution and on-pack promotions to convert match-day attention into purchases. It fits Unilever's broader influencer-first shift, which moves influencer spend from roughly 30% to 50% of its total ad budget.

title: How to rank in AI search results: Expert best practices
blurb: AI search visitors convert at 4.4x the rate of standard organic traffic according to Semrush, and Ahrefs found they drove 12.1% of signups while making up just 0.5% of total traffic. With AI Overviews now appearing in roughly 48% of tracked Google searches and ChatGPT handling over a billion queries weekly, the teams building AEO citation authority now are doing it while competition is still low. The playbook starts with opening your site to AI crawlers, then structuring content around the specific prompts buyers actually ask.

title: AI Slop Accounts for 59% of TikToks Shown to New Users, per Report
blurb: Kapwing found AI slop made up 59% of the first 500 TikToks shown to a new account, and 57% in TikTok's Kids category.

title: Anthropic ships major Claude Design overhaul with design system imports, code round-trips, and a fix for its token-burning problem
blurb: Anthropic shipped a major update to Claude Design that lets teams import design systems from GitHub repos, design files, or uploads, then builds and auto-corrects components against those rules while round-tripping to code. The release also targets the tool's heavy token consumption, repositioning a viral research preview that hit 1M users in a week from a prototyping demo into an enterprise brand-compliance layer.

title: OpenAI launches scheduled tasks in ChatGPT, details here
blurb: OpenAI began rolling out scheduled tasks in ChatGPT, letting users set the assistant to run prompts at recurring times and deliver results automatically. The feature pushes ChatGPT toward proactive, agent-style workflows, giving PMs a built-in option for routine reports and monitoring that previously required a standalone automation tool.

title: How to design AI agent loops: schedules, goals, and subagents in Claude Code and Codex
blurb: The author breaks down four loop types for AI coding agents — heartbeat, cron, hook, and goal — and argues a loop is just an automated prompt, then names the five things any production loop needs: work trees, skills, plugins, subagents, and state tracking. He builds two live examples: a daily aging-PR reviewer in Claude Code that self-schedules and spawns subagents, and a weekly skills-identification loop in Codex whose goal-based subagents validate their own output. The practical warning for PMs: goal loops are the hardest to write and the fastest way to burn tokens for nothing.

title: The Mom-and-Pop SaaS era has arrived
blurb: Elena Verna argues the bigger AI shift isn't faster developers but a collapse in the cost of building software, which makes entirely new categories economically viable. As building gets cheap, the winners will be domain experts (teachers, real estate agents, accountants, coaches) shipping "Mom-and-Pop SaaS" for problems they understand deeply, rather than a small group of venture-funded engineers in a few tech hubs.

title: Ethereum's Glamsterdam Upgrade Enters Final Devnet Phase With 200M Gas-Limit Target
blurb: Ethereum's Glamsterdam hard fork reached its final devnet stage, locking a 10-EIP bundle headlined by EIP-7732 (enshrined Proposer-Builder Separation) and EIP-7928 (Block-Level Access Lists) ahead of mainnet activation in the second half of 2026. The pairing clears the way for a 200M gas-limit floor, roughly tripling current L1 capacity from the 60M range and targeting up to 10,000 TPS-equivalent throughput under realistic workloads.

title: Illinois Enacts the Strictest Digital-Asset Tax in the US as Industry Group Urges Veto
blurb: Illinois Governor JB Pritzker signed SB 3019, the Digital Asset Privilege Tax Act, making Illinois the first state to levy a 0.2% tax on every digital-asset exchange, transfer, or custody event rather than on income or gains. The Crypto Council for Innovation is urging a line-item veto of the provision, warning it singles out crypto for treatment with no equivalent on stocks or bonds and could trigger a 50-state patchwork of conflicting tax regimes.

title: Coinbase Stakes Out Brokerage Territory With SEC-Registered AI Advisor and Stock Options Push
blurb: Coinbase used its latest "System Update" to push into retail-brokerage territory, launching an SEC-, CFTC-, and NFA-registered AI advisor, zero-commission stock and ETF trading on Advanced, and forthcoming equity and crypto options. The bundle, which the company frames as building "the Everything Exchange," shifts Coinbase's competitive set from Binance and Kraken toward Robinhood, Schwab, and Interactive Brokers.

title: Microsoft found malware that hijacks crypto wallets and spreads through USB sticks
blurb: Microsoft identified a USB-spreading worm called Trojan:Win32/CryptoBandits that has been harvesting seed phrases, private keys, and recipient addresses from the Windows clipboard since February, exfiltrating the data over Tor. The "crypto clipper" also silently swaps in attacker-controlled wallet addresses during transfers, and Microsoft urged users to disable AutoRun and block .lnk execution on removable media.

title: Congress Strikes Housing-Bill Deal That Bans Fed CBDC Through 2030
blurb: Congressional negotiators folded a statutory ban on a Federal Reserve retail CBDC into the bipartisan 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act (H.R. 6644), barring any Fed-issued digital dollar through December 31, 2030. The text carves out private, dollar-denominated stablecoins, slotting alongside the GENIUS Act framework and leaving issuers on payment rails to compete for years with no Fed product in the same lane.

title: Gaming Industry, Tribes and Unions Press Senate to Ban Sports Prediction Markets in Crypto Bill
blurb: A coalition led by the American Gaming Association, tribal groups, and hospitality unions urged the Senate to amend the CLARITY Act to bar prediction markets from offering sports event contracts, directly targeting Kalshi and Polymarket. The June 16 letter argues such contracts evade state and tribal gaming rules, citing an AGA estimate of roughly $1B in lost state tax revenue since early 2025.

title: Kalshi Eyes Broader Asset Classes for Perpetual Futures After $5.5B Crypto Launch
blurb: Kalshi's CFTC-regulated perpetual futures crossed $5.5B in trading volume in their first two weeks, less than a month after the commission approved its bitcoin perpetual contract on May 29. Co-founder Tarek Mansour said the exchange is in talks with regulators to extend perps beyond its 11 crypto contracts into commodities, equity indexes, and FX, a path that would require case-by-case review under Regulation 40.3.

title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
blurb: Microsoft detected a self-propagating worm it calls Crypto Clipper that spreads via USB .lnk files and scans clipboards for wallet addresses and seed phrases, exfiltrating credentials and screenshots to attacker servers over a portable Tor client routed through a local SOCKS5 proxy. The malware blends data theft with remote code execution, functioning as a lightweight backdoor without traditional installers or exposed IP-based command-and-control infrastructure.

title: Matter Labs Cuts Staff, Pivots Fully to Institutional Privacy Platform Prividium
blurb: Matter Labs, the company behind the zkSync Ethereum layer-2, cut staff and committed the entire organization to Prividium, an institutional on-chain privacy and compliance stack it began building in 2024 for regulated financial institutions. The pivot follows ZK consolidations at Hyli and Botanix, reflecting pressure on general-purpose L2s to find defensible verticals where regulated entities pay for compliance tooling.

title: U.S. agencies seek stablecoin customer-ID rules akin to banks in new GENIUS Act pitch
blurb: The Federal Reserve, Treasury, OCC, FDIC and NCUA released a proposed rule requiring stablecoin issuers to verify customer identities under Bank Secrecy Act standards, the latest step in implementing the GENIUS Act. The agencies opened a 60-day comment period, signaling that US issuers will face bank-grade KYC and sanctions-screening obligations rather than a lighter crypto-specific regime.

title: Zama, Morpho and Steakhouse Open First Confidential USDC Yield Vault on Ethereum
blurb: Zama, Morpho, and Steakhouse Financial opened the first confidential USDC yield vault on Ethereum, with deposits starting June 23 through a Morpho-built vault curated by Steakhouse that accepts cUSDC, Zama's fully homomorphic encryption wrapper of Circle's stablecoin. Balances, deposit sizes, and transfer values stay encrypted on-chain while the vault routes capital into Steakhouse's existing Prime v2 strategy backed by cbBTC, WBTC, and wstETH.

title: Malta's financial regulator explores bringing parts of DeFi under MiCA's orbit
blurb: Malta's Financial Services Authority opened a discussion paper exploring how DeFi could fall under the EU's MiCA framework, proposing that decentralization be assessed as a spectrum rather than a binary given that many protocols retain admin keys, concentrated governance, and upgrade rights. The MFSA is seeking feedback through July 10 on a standardized test for when a protocol sits outside MiCA's scope.

title: USB worm spreads crypto-stealing malware via Windows shortcut files
blurb: Microsoft detailed a USB worm active since February that spreads clipper malware via Windows LNK shortcut files, swapping copied wallet addresses for attacker-controlled ones and scanning clipboards every half-second for BIP39 seed phrases, Ethereum private keys, and Bitcoin and Tron addresses. The malware stages payloads from a .onion address, exfiltrates screenshots and keys over Tor, and supports remote code execution via a C2 EVAL instruction.

title: Alchemy's AI-driven identity and payment service gains access to Visa network
blurb: Alchemy connected its AgentCard identity and payment service to the Visa network, letting AI agents built on any model provider transact with Visa-issued tokens while preserving rewards, credit lines, and card benefits without new credentials. Each agent also receives a dedicated email address and phone number, joining Stripe, Visa, and Mastercard in the early push into agentic commerce.

title: Algorand unveils roadmap to achieve quantum resistance by 2028
blurb: The Algorand Foundation released a roadmap to make its blockchain broadly quantum-resistant during 2027, starting in 2026 with post-quantum accounts, multisignature wallets, and staking support before extending protection to core protocol components. The plan joins similar quantum-preparedness efforts at Ethereum and Solana, all aimed at migrating away from elliptic curve cryptography well ahead of any "Q-Day" capable of breaking it.

title: Digital credit market hit by huge selloff as Strive CEO blames leverage liquidations
blurb: The digital credit market suffered one of its sharpest selloffs to date on Thursday, with Strategy's STRC preferred equity falling to $82.50 before recovering to $89 and Strive's SATA dropping below $93 before rebounding to $97, both well off their $100 par targets. Strive CEO Matt Cole attributed the move to a leverage liquidation event driven by margin calls and forced selling rather than any deterioration in issuers' credit quality.

title: Ex-Celsius CEO Mashinsky gets U.S. CFTC ban in final resolution with regulator
blurb: The CFTC permanently banned former Celsius CEO Alexander Mashinsky from commodities registration and trading, closing its years-long fraud case without adding new fines. The order layers onto his 2025 criminal sentence of 12 years in prison, a $50,000 fine, and $48M in restitution for misleading hundreds of thousands of customers about Celsius' solvency before its 2022 collapse.

title: The CFTC permanently bans Celsius founder Alex Mashinsky from trading in markets it oversees as part of a settlement resolving its 2023 lawsuit against him (Jesse Coghlan/Cointelegraph)
blurb: The CFTC permanently banned Celsius founder Alex Mashinsky from trading in any market it regulates, settling its 2023 fraud suit tied to the lender's collapse. The trading ban runs alongside Mashinsky's separate criminal sentence and closes the agency's civil case over Celsius's misrepresentation of its risk and token-buyback practices.

title: Backpack's Tokenized SpaceX Token on Solana Crosses 10,000 Holders, Nearly Double xStocks' SPCXx
blurb: Backpack's tokenized SpaceX share token SPCX crossed 10,000 onchain holders on Solana six days after listing, roughly double the 3,000 holders on Backed Finance's rival xStocks SPCXx. SPCX, issued by broker-dealer Backpack Securities and backed one-for-one by custodied shares redeemable via ACATS and DTCC rails, has cleared over $350M in cumulative volume since going live.

title: World Chain Bridge TVL Climbs 33% Over Seven Days as Worldcoin Token Posts Matching Rally
blurb: World Chain's canonical bridge TVL rose 32.87% over seven days to about $602M, making it the Optimism Stack rollup's deepest collateral pool, with WLD up over 50% in the same window. Most of the bridged stack sits idle from a yield perspective, with active deployment concentrated in Re7 Labs vaults on Morpho Blue, which hold $32.69M, roughly 35% of the curator's cross-chain book.

title: Live markets: Bitcoin has traded below its mining cost for five months, squeezing miners
blurb: Bitcoin has traded below its ~$78,000 production cost for five straight months, JPMorgan said, pushing roughly 20% of miners into unprofitability and forcing public miners to sell more than 32,000 BTC in Q1 to cover operating costs. Network difficulty dropped 10% in early June as higher-cost rigs powered down, and JPMorgan expects larger, more frequent adjustments while bitcoin stays near $62,500.

title: Court-blocked: ASIC wins appeal against Block Earner over financial product regulation
blurb: Australia's High Court ruled 7-0 for ASIC, finding that Block Earner's defunct Earner yield product was an unlicensed financial product, ending four years of litigation and overturning the Full Federal Court's earlier decision for the crypto platform. The ruling confirms that crypto products promising a return fall under Australia's existing financial services regime, giving ASIC clear precedent to require licensing without new legislation.

title: Ethereum Foundation loses another key leader as co-executive director Hsiao-Wei Wang resigns
blurb: Ethereum Foundation co-executive director Hsiao-Wei Wang resigned effective immediately, following fellow co-executive director Tomasz Stańczak out the door and bringing senior departures to at least eight over the past five months. Board member Bastian Aue is steering the organization in the interim as the EF faces mounting community scrutiny of its governance and strategy amid competition from rival chains.

title: The money flow is the moat
blurb: The author argues that the most durable businesses sit "in the money flow," taking a cut of value moving through a network, and that crypto is the first technology built natively for this pattern. Stablecoins extend it to any startup by letting money settle globally and 24/7 with end-to-end programmability, while network tokens align users, developers, and validators around growing the same shared ledger. The piece frames open rails and public unit economics as the new moat.

title: Strategy's STRC Falls to Record Low, Squeezing a Bitcoin Funding Channel
blurb: Strategy's STRC preferred stock fell to a 52-week low near $85 on Thursday, roughly 15% below its $100 stated amount and lifting the effective yield to about 13.5% against the 11.50% declared rate. The discount squeezes the at-the-market program Strategy uses to fund bitcoin buys, raising less cash per share just two weeks after the company sold BTC for the first time since 2022 to cover preferred dividends.

title: Hive shares jumps 10% on $220m Canada sovereign AI infrastructure deal
blurb: HIVE Digital Technologies shares jumped 10% in pre-market trading after the bitcoin miner signed a $220M, three-year GPU cloud contract with Bell Canada and Cohere to deploy 2,304 Nvidia Grace Blackwell GPUs for sovereign AI infrastructure in British Columbia. The deal adds roughly $70M in annual recurring revenue, pushing HIVE's contracted HPC revenue target above $100M as it pivots away from pure-play mining.

title: Ondo Finance Adds 173 Tokenized Stocks and ETFs, Taking Catalog Past 430 Assets Across Three Chains
blurb: Ondo Finance added 173 tokenized stocks and ETFs to Ondo Global Markets, pushing its catalog past 430 assets live simultaneously across Ethereum, Solana, and BNB Chain. The batch includes BlackRock active ETFs and covered-call income strategies, each token backed 1:1 by the underlying security held with a US broker-dealer, on a platform that crossed $1B in TVL and holds over 70% of the tokenized-equity market.

title: Bitcoin traders load up on bearish bets all the way down to $52,000
blurb: Bitcoin options traders piled into short- and near-dated out-of-the-money puts on Deribit over the past 24 to 48 hours, with strikes spanning $61,500 down to $52,000 across June 22 to July 31 expirations. The hedging surge tracks a hawkish Fed, persistent ETF outflows, and pressure on Strategy, whose STRC preferred stock has fallen well below its $100 par value as BTC trades near $62,400.

title: Illinois Enacts the Strictest Digital-Asset Tax in the US as Industry Group Urges Veto
blurb: Illinois Governor JB Pritzker signed SB 3019, the Digital Asset Privilege Tax Act, imposing a 0.2% levy on every digital-asset exchange, transfer, and custody event, the first US state tax tied to transactions rather than income or gains. The Crypto Council for Innovation is urging a line-item veto, warning that the law applies even to self-transfers between wallets and could trigger a fifty-state patchwork that undercuts the federal framework Congress is drafting.

title: Coinbase Stakes Out Brokerage Territory With SEC-Registered AI Advisor and Stock Options Push
blurb: Coinbase used its latest System Update to roll out an SEC-registered AI investment advisor, zero-commission stock and ETF trading on Coinbase Advanced, and forthcoming equity and crypto options, reframing its rivals from Binance and Kraken to Robinhood, Schwab, and Interactive Brokers. The "Everything Exchange" push pulls Coinbase directly into retail-brokerage territory, with tokenized US stocks for non-US customers and a unified global liquidity pool merging its spot, derivatives, and Deribit venues still to come.

title: Congress Strikes Housing-Bill Deal That Bans Fed CBDC Through 2030
blurb: Congressional negotiators folded a statutory ban on a Federal Reserve retail CBDC into the bipartisan 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, blocking any Fed-issued digital dollar through December 31, 2030. The text carves out private dollar-denominated stablecoins, slotting alongside the GENIUS Act framework and clearing a lane for issuers building on payment rails without competition from a central-bank product for years.

title: Gaming Industry, Tribes and Unions Press Senate to Ban Sports Prediction Markets in Crypto Bill
blurb: A coalition led by the American Gaming Association, joined by tribal groups and the AFL-CIO's hospitality unions, urged the Senate to use the CLARITY Act to bar prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket from offering sports wagers. The June 16 letter argues sports event contracts are an end-run around state and tribal gaming rules, citing roughly $1B in lost state tax revenue since 2025, a figure operators dispute.

title: Kalshi Eyes Broader Asset Classes for Perpetual Futures After $5.5B Crypto Launch
blurb: Kalshi's CFTC-regulated perpetual futures crossed $5.5B in trading volume in their first two weeks, and the exchange is now in talks with regulators about extending the contracts beyond crypto into commodities, equity indexes, and FX. The expansion would run through the CFTC's case-by-case Regulation 40.3 review and reposition Kalshi from a prediction-market venue into a multi-asset derivatives exchange.

title: Malta's financial regulator explores bringing parts of DeFi under MiCA's orbit
blurb: Malta's MFSA published a discussion paper exploring how DeFi could fall under the EU's MiCA framework, proposing that decentralization be assessed as a spectrum rather than a binary, since most protocols retain centralized features like admin keys and governance concentration. The paper also asks whether regulated crypto firms should run smart-contract audits and governance reviews before integrating DeFi protocols, with responses open until July 10.

title: Zama, Morpho and Steakhouse Open First Confidential USDC Yield Vault on Ethereum
blurb: Zama, Morpho, and Steakhouse Financial are launching the first DeFi yield product for Confidential USDC, with deposits opening June 23 through a Morpho vault that routes cUSDC into Steakhouse's existing Prime v2 strategy. The vault uses fully homomorphic encryption to keep balances, deposit sizes, and transfer values encrypted on Ethereum while the curator still reads aggregate solvency, extending Zama's privacy wrapper from transfers into yield-bearing lending for the first time.

title: Ex-Celsius CEO Mashinsky gets U.S. CFTC ban in final resolution with regulator
blurb: The CFTC formally banned former Celsius CEO Alexander Mashinsky from registration and commodities trading, closing its years-long fraud case without adding new fines. The civil resolution layers onto his criminal sentence of 12 years in prison, a $50,000 fine, and $48M in restitution for misleading hundreds of thousands of customers about the safety of Celsius before its 2022 collapse.

title: 3 ex-Palantir engineers raise $60m from Index, Iconiq, SAP for agentic operating system
blurb: Conduct, a London-based startup founded by three ex-Palantir engineers, raised $60M in Series A funding led by Index Ventures and Iconiq, less than nine months after its $11M seed. SAP took a strategic stake and is embedding Conduct's agentic AI operating system, which helps enterprises like DHL and Fraport untangle decades of custom business logic, directly into its products.

title: Mukesh Ambani Says Jio Platforms to File IPO Papers Friday
blurb: Jio Platforms, the digital and telecom arm of Mukesh Ambani's Reliance Industries, filed draft listing documents with India's market regulator Friday, kicking off one of the country's most anticipated IPOs. The listing tees up a public valuation for a business spanning mobile, broadband, and digital payments, giving Reliance a path to unlock shareholder value across its consumer-finance and fintech stack.

title: U.S. agencies seek stablecoin customer-ID rules akin to banks in new GENIUS Act pitch
blurb: The Federal Reserve, Treasury, OCC, FDIC and NCUA jointly proposed a rule requiring stablecoin issuers to verify customer identities under Bank Secrecy Act standards, the latest step implementing the GENIUS Act. The proposal mandates KYC procedures, identity recordkeeping and terrorist-list screening, and opens a 60-day comment period that pulls stablecoin issuers further into the same AML compliance regime as banks and brokerages.

title: Algorand unveils roadmap to achieve quantum resistance by 2028
blurb: The Algorand Foundation outlined a roadmap to make its blockchain quantum-resistant by the end of 2027, starting with post-quantum accounts, multisignature wallets, and staking support in 2026 before reworking core protocol components. The phased timeline reflects a broader industry recognition, shared by Ethereum and Solana, that migrating live cryptographic infrastructure off elliptic-curve schemes must begin years ahead of any viable quantum threat.

title: Digital credit market hit by huge selloff as Strive CEO blames leverage liquidations
blurb: Strive Asset Management's STRC and SATA preferred products sold off sharply on Thursday, with STRC falling to $82.50 and SATA below $93 before both rebounded toward their $100 par values. CEO Matt Cole attributed the drop to a leverage liquidation event from margin calls and forced selling rather than deteriorating credit quality, comparing it to past blowups in leveraged Treasury trades.

title: Alchemy's AI-driven identity and payment service gains access to Visa network
blurb: Alchemy connected its AgentCard identity and payment product to Visa's Intelligent Commerce network, letting AI agents built on any model transact using Visa-issued tokens while preserving rewards, credit lines, and card benefits. Each AgentCard also provisions a dedicated email address and phone number, giving agents a full identity layer to sign up for services, clear verifications, and check out without the consumer touching a payment screen.

title: Collapsed Lender MFS Said to Have Never Registered £300 Million of Mortgages
blurb: A £300M ($396M) mortgage portfolio tied to collapsed UK lender Market Financial Solutions was apparently never registered with public property records, according to court filings in a petition to liquidate portfolio holder Twinwin Ltd. Without registration, which establishes a lender's priority claim over a property, creditors face a difficult fight to prove their interest in the underlying assets during liquidation.

title: What Link data tells us about AI spending
blurb: Stripe's analysis of 250M Link wallet customers found the top 10% of AI spenders nearly doubled their monthly AI outlay from $183 in December 2025 to $359 in March 2026, with app-builder platforms like Replit and Lovable up 5x since January 2025. Stripe is tying the data to its new Link wallet for agents, which lets customers authorize AI agents to transact on their behalf with preset spending controls across any Stripe seller.

title: Backpack's Tokenized SpaceX Token on Solana Crosses 10,000 Holders, Nearly Double xStocks' SPCXx
blurb: Backpack's tokenized SpaceX share token, SPCX, crossed 10,000 onchain holders on Solana six days after listing, roughly double the 3,000 holders on rival xStocks' SPCXx. Each SPL token is backed one-for-one by a SpaceX share custodied in a Backpack Securities broker-dealer account and redeemable into a traditional brokerage via ACATS and DTCC rails, with cumulative volume passing $350M.

title: Filing: Jio Platforms, India's largest wireless operator with 526M+ subscribers, files for an IPO; the deal is expected to be one of India's biggest IPOs ever (Priyanka Salve/CNBC)
blurb: Jio Platforms, India's largest wireless operator with over 526M subscribers, filed for an IPO that is expected to rank among the largest in India's history. The listing gives Reliance-backed Jio a public-market currency to fund its push deeper into digital payments, lending, and financial services across India's still-underbanked consumer base.

title: PayPal Ventures shutters as company restructuring continues
blurb: PayPal is winding down PayPal Ventures, its corporate venture arm founded in 2016, pausing new investments and exploring secondary sales of holdings through Jefferies. The move under CEO Enrique Lores severs PayPal's front-row view into early fintech startups like Plaid and Anchorage Digital, just as competitors retain venture arms to track emerging payment and AI infrastructure.

title: Court-blocked: ASIC wins appeal against Block Earner over financial product regulation
blurb: Australia's High Court ruled 7-0 that Block Earner's defunct Earner yield product was a financial product, handing ASIC a win after four years of litigation and overturning a Full Federal Court decision in the platform's favor. The ruling confirms that crypto products promising a return fall under Australia's existing financial services regime, requiring an AFSL, and signals ASIC will police yield-bearing digital asset offerings without new legislation.

title: Investors Anticipate Fresh Liquidity Following Historic SpaceX Debut
blurb: SpaceX's public debut sets up a long-awaited distribution event for private markets, freeing locked-up capital as a wave of multibillion-dollar AI companies line up to IPO. Wellington's late-stage growth head Matt Witheiler expects the proceeds to recirculate into the private tech ecosystem, easing a liquidity drought that has starved venture and growth funds of fresh cash.

title: Flagright secures $12.5M Series A to scale AI compliance platform
blurb: Flagright raised a $12.5M Series A led by Infinity Ventures, with participation from Sella Direct Ventures and continued backing from Frontline and Y Combinator, to scale its AI operating system for financial crime compliance. The capital funds an expansion of explainable AI across transaction monitoring, watchlist screening, and case management, plus a deeper US push as institutions replace rigid legacy systems with auditable, AI-native compliance tooling.

title: FFinRED: An Expert-Guided Benchmark Generation and Evaluation Framework for Financial LLM Red-Teaming
blurb: This paper introduces FinRED, an expert-guided red-teaming framework for evaluating financial LLMs against finance-specific risks like regulatory evasion, fraud facilitation, and compliance violations. Its two-level taxonomy maps standards such as FATF and EU DORA to threats, and a finance-tuned rubric cut critical false negatives from 28 to 12 versus generic safety checks. FinRED is already deployed in South Korea's Financial Security Institute regulatory sandbox, with the dataset and pipeline gated to qualified researchers to limit dual-use.

title: Hive shares jumps 10% on $220m Canada sovereign AI infrastructure deal
blurb: HIVE Digital Technologies signed a $220M, three-year contract with Bell Canada and Cohere to deploy 2,304 Nvidia Grace Blackwell GPUs in British Columbia, accelerating its pivot from bitcoin mining to AI compute. The deal adds roughly $70M in annual recurring revenue and pushes HIVE's contracted HPC revenue above $100M, with all infrastructure kept on Canadian soil to support Ottawa's sovereign-AI push.

title: UK Digital Bank Starling Shrinks Board as McPike Cements Control
blurb: Starling Bank is shrinking its board as several directors exit and the digital bank declines to replace most of them, concentrating influence in the hands of largest shareholder Harald McPike. The streamlining tightens McPike's grip over a UK neobank still weighing an eventual IPO, raising governance questions as power consolidates around a single dominant investor ahead of any public listing.
